tool_args.label: New York City Transit Key Performance Metrics - September 2022

payload.title: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.description: Key performance metrics report prepared for the September 2022 meeting of the New York City Transit & Bus Committee

evidence_refs.source_quote: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
September 2022
This performance metrics document was prepared for the September 2022 meeting of the New York City Transit & Bus Committee.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.raw_text: New York City Transit

evidence_refs.source_quote: New York City Transit and MTA Bus operate all subways and buses in New York City.

evidence_refs.source_quote: We continue to work with our partners at NYC Department of Transportation to expand bus priority throughout the city this year.

tool_args.label: NYCT Committee Members
tool_args.raw_text: Members of the New York City Transit Committee include: • Haeda Mihaltses, Chair • Andrew Albert • Jamey Barbas • Randy Glucksman • David Jones • Frankie Miranda • Robert Mujica • John Samuelsen • Sherif Soliman • Lisa Sorin • Midori Valdivia

payload.description: Members: Haeda Mihaltses (Chair), Andrew Albert, Jamey Barbas, Randy Glucksman, David Jones, Frankie Miranda, Robert Mujica, John Samuelsen, Sherif Soliman, Lisa Sorin, Midori Valdivia

tool_args.label: New York City Transit Committee
tool_args.raw_text: Members of the New York City Transit Committee include: • Haeda Mihaltses, Chair • Andrew Albert • Jamey Barbas • Randy Glucksman • David Jones • Frankie Miranda • Robert Mujica • John Samuelsen • Sherif Soliman • Lisa Sorin • Midori Valdivia

tool_args.label: Queens Bus Network Redesign
tool_args.raw_text: The Queens Bus Network Redesign seeks to improve commutes by simplifying routes, balancing bus stops to improve speeds, increasing off-peak service and enhancing connectivity to the subway system and other bus routes.

payload.description: The Queens Bus Network Redesign team hosted 40 events over the past five months to gather feedback on the new draft plan. Final plan expected to be published in 2023.

evidence_refs.source_quote: In Queens, the Bus Network Redesign team hosted 40 events over the past five months to gather feedback from bus customers and other stakeholders on the new draft plan.

The Queens Bus Network Redesign seeks to improve commutes by simplifying routes, balancing bus stops to improve speeds, increasing off-peak service and enhancing connectivity to the subway system and other bus routes.

tool_args.label: Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign
tool_args.raw_text: The Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign included adding frequency to 10 bus routes, thereby reducing customer wait times.

payload.description: The Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign included adding frequency to 10 bus routes. The redesigned network launched on June 26.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign included adding frequency to 10 bus routes, thereby reducing customer wait times.

tool_args.label: A Line Service Delivery and Reliability Initiative
tool_args.raw_text: The Department of Subways is piloting a service delivery and reliability initiative on the A Line that safely increases speed limits in various segments to match modern NYCT engineering policy.

payload.project_name: A Line Service Delivery and Reliability Initiative
payload.description: Pilot on the A Line that safely increases speed limits in various segments, placing supervision and platform personnel at Dyckman Street, and a dedicated supervisor at 207 Street.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Department of Subways is piloting a service delivery and reliability initiative on the A Line that safely increases speed limits in various segments to match modern NYCT engineering policy.

tool_args.label: Main Street Busway (Permanent)
tool_args.raw_text: The Main Street busways was also declared permanent after a successful pilot launched in 2021.

payload.description: Main Street busway declared permanent after a successful pilot launched in 2021.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Main Street busways was also declared permanent after a successful pilot launched in 2021.

tool_args.label: Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) Expansion
tool_args.raw_text: We are excited to announce the expansion of Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) with the first of 300 additional equipped buses covering nine routes to be implemented starting in September on the Q44 SBS route.

payload.description: Expansion of ABLE with 300 additional equipped buses covering nine routes, starting September on Q44 SBS.

evidence_refs.source_quote: We are excited to announce the expansion of Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) with the first of 300 additional equipped buses covering nine routes to be implemented starting in September on the Q44 SBS route.

tool_args.label: Open Stroller Pilot
tool_args.raw_text: We are pleased to announce that we are launching a pilot this month to allow open strollers on select NYCT and MTA bus routes in each borough.

payload.description: 6-month pilot to allow open strollers on select NYCT and MTA bus routes. Routes: M31, B1, Q12, Q50, Bx23, S53 and S93.

evidence_refs.source_quote: We are pleased to announce that we are launching a pilot this month to allow open strollers on select NYCT and MTA bus routes in each borough.

tool_args.label: TransitTalk at Church B/Q Station
tool_args.raw_text: On August 25th, customers were greeted by President Rich Davey, members of his executive team, and customer service representatives to discuss issues specific to their daily station experience and commute.

payload.description: President Rich Davey and team met customers at Church B/Q station to discuss issues and gather feedback.

evidence_refs.source_quote: On August 25th, customers were greeted by President Rich Davey, members of his executive team, and customer service representatives to discuss issues specific to their daily station experience and commute.

warnings: event.participants is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Participant lists refer to entity records. Suggested relation: has_participant.

tool_args.label: OMNY Help Desk at 161st St Yankee Stadium
tool_args.raw_text: On July 15th, as the NY Yankees prepared to play the Boston Red Sox, representatives from MTA set up an OMNY Help Desk at 161st St-Yankee Stadium

payload.description: MTA set up an OMNY Help Desk at 161st St-Yankee Stadium to promote and demonstrate the benefits of MTA's contactless fare payment system. Bronx Borough President Vanessa Gibson joined.

evidence_refs.source_quote: On July 15th, as the NY Yankees prepared to play the Boston Red Sox, representatives from MTA set up an OMNY Help Desk at 161st St-Yankee Stadium to promote and demonstrate the benefits and convenience of using MTA's contactless fare payment system.

tool_args.label: University Avenue Bus Lanes (Bronx)
tool_args.raw_text: over five miles of new bus lanes have been added along three corridors – University Avenue in the Bronx, Avenue A and Avenue D in Manhattan, and 21st Street in Queens.

tool_args.label: 21st Street Bus Lanes (Queens)
tool_args.raw_text: over five miles of new bus lanes have been added along three corridors – University Avenue in the Bronx, Avenue A and Avenue D in Manhattan, and 21st Street in Queens.

tool_args.label: Q44 SBS (ABLE expansion route)
tool_args.raw_text: the first of 300 additional equipped buses covering nine routes to be implemented starting in September on the Q44 SBS route

evidence_refs.source_quote: the first of 300 additional equipped buses covering nine routes to be implemented starting in September on the Q44 SBS route

tool_args.label: M125 (Bronx-Manhattan)
tool_args.raw_text: We also implemented a new permanent routing of the southbound M125 traveling from the Bronx to Manhattan.

payload.description: New permanent routing of the southbound M125 traveling from the Bronx to Manhattan. First M125 stop shared with the last southbound Bx15 stop on 3rd Avenue.

evidence_refs.source_quote: We also implemented a new permanent routing of the southbound M125 traveling from the Bronx to Manhattan.

tool_args.raw_text: 93% Subway Service Delivered in August

evidence_refs.source_quote: Subway Service Delivered in August

tool_args.raw_text: 85% Subway CJTP in August - Highest Since May 2021

tool_args.label: Bus Speed August 2022 (AM Peak)
tool_args.raw_text: Speeds remained above average at 8.2 mph in both July and August, up 1% compared to pre-Covid levels. The AM Peak (between 6am and 10 am) was particularly high at 8.5 mph in August, up 2.5% compared to pre-COVID AM peak levels.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The AM Peak (between 6am and 10 am) was particularly high at 8.5 mph in August, up 2.5% compared to pre-COVID AM peak levels.

tool_args.label: Bus Service Delivered August 2022
tool_args.raw_text: Bus Service Delivered has remained above 95% since June, and is up 1.9% compared to August last year.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Bus Service Delivered has remained above 95% since June, and is up 1.9% compared to August last year.

issues: metric_claim payload with raw_value_text should include normalized numeric value or value_min/value_max

tool_args.label: Bus CJTP August 2022
tool_args.raw_text: CJTP has shown steady improvement for three consecutive months. Performance at 77.1% in August was slightly higher than the previous month and August last year, and up 1.3% compared to pre-Covid levels.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Performance at 771% in August
